properties.name: ogiri.security.register-filter
properties.description: Enable automatic registration of the ogiri SecurityFilterChain bean. Set to false to manually configure security filters.

properties.name: ogiri.auth.register-token-service
properties.description: Enable auto-registration of the default OgiriTokenService. Set to false to provide a custom token service.

properties.name: ogiri.auth.max-clients
properties.description: Maximum number of active APP tokens per user. When exceeded, the oldest token is revoked.

properties.name: ogiri.auth.batch-grace-seconds
properties.description: Grace period (seconds) for detecting batch requests. Requests within this window don't trigger token rotation.

properties.name: ogiri.auth.token-lifespan-days
properties.description: Default token lifetime in days. New tokens will expire after this duration.

properties.name: ogiri.auth.rotate-on-write-only
properties.description: Only rotate tokens on mutating HTTP requests (POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH). GET requests update lastUsedAt but don't trigger rotation.

properties.name: ogiri.auth.rotate-stale-seconds
properties.description: Force token rotation if token exceeds this age (seconds). 0 = disabled, no staleness-based rotation.

properties.name: ogiri.cleanup.enabled
properties.description: Enable the scheduled OgiriTokenCleanupJob to automatically delete expired tokens.

properties.name: ogiri.cleanup.interval-ms
properties.description: Interval in milliseconds between cleanup job executions. Default is 6 hours (21600000).

values.description: Relaxed: suitable for users with many devices
Default: balance between rotation and efficiency
Default: balance between security and user experience
Disabled: rely only on batch window logic
6 hours: default, good for most production use cases
